review_basis.source: AWS IAM Access Analyzer policy validation

scope_classes.global_required: Resource wildcard remains expected for account-level, list, introspection, or create-before-ARN operations in thesis-demo-v1.
scope_classes.prefix_scope_candidate: Action supports resource-level scoping or predictable names, but the exact ARN and naming contract still need supervised cloud validation before hardening.
scope_classes.conditioned: Statement is broad only where paired with an IAM condition that constrains the target service.

statements.reason: Resource group creation/deletion can be narrowed once the final naming prefix and destroy behavior are validated.
IAM role tagging should eventually target thesis-managed role ARNs only.
Terraform creates /aws/lambda, /aws/states, and /aws/iot log groups; log-group ARN scoping should use the final deployment prefix after live validation.
Role lifecycle actions should eventually target only thesis-managed IAM role ARNs.
Lambda operations should eventually target thesis-managed function ARNs.
IoT thing, certificate, policy, and rule resources need provider validation before prefix-scoped ARNs are enforced.
sts:GetCallerIdentity is an account identity introspection call and remains wildcard-scoped.
State-machine actions should eventually target thesis-managed state machine ARNs.
DynamoDB table and backup actions should eventually target thesis-managed table/backup ARNs.
Bucket/object actions should eventually target thesis-managed bucket names and object prefixes.
EventBridge rule/target actions should eventually target thesis-managed rule ARNs.
Workspace/entity/component operations need live validation for exact supported ARN patterns.
Managed Grafana workspace actions should eventually target thesis-managed workspaces where AWS supports resource-level scoping.
IAM Identity Center discovery calls are account/region-level setup checks for Managed Grafana.
Identity Store user lifecycle operations need supervised validation against the final Grafana user-provisioning flow.
iam:PassRole remains wildcard-scoped in thesis-demo-v1 but is constrained to lambda.amazonaws.com via iam:PassedToService.
Credential self-inspection must read the caller's attached and inline policies before deployment preflight can classify readiness.

known_gaps: Resource ARN scoping is documented but not enforced until supervised cloud validation confirms exact name and destroy behavior.
AWS Access Analyzer validate-policy is documented as a required manual provider-validation command; it is not run in default tests because it requires live AWS credentials.
E2E deployment verification is intentionally deferred until the larger refactoring roadmap reaches an end-to-end testable state.
next_validation: Run AWS IAM Access Analyzer validate-policy against aws_deployer_policy.json.
Run a supervised AWS deployment/destroy smoke with thesis-demo-v1 credentials.
Capture CloudTrail or IAM Access Analyzer generated-policy evidence to produce least-privilege-v2.
